Barrel Sauna UK
Commercial Proposal · Prepared for Dan Breeze

What we're building

A complete outdoor wellness suite, built to one spec

Everything here is made to order in our Caerphilly workshop and finished in the same 48mm treated thermowood, so the sauna, hot tub, plunges and shower read as one considered set rather than a collection of parts. The materials, textures and tones carry across every piece.

  • Six-metre barrel sauna, 2.3m wide, potential capacity up to twenty
  • One-metre changing room as an integrated heat and sound trap, with a silent glass inner door
  • Panoramic dome window at the far end, seating built into the globe
  • Harvia Cilindro WiFi heater, around ninety kilograms of stone, programmable on and off through the day
  • Under-seat LED lighting, cool in the changing room and warm in the sauna
  • Hot tub with hydro jets and colour-changing LED, sized for relaxed use
  • Two double cold plunges, each seats two and independently set, one bracing and one a gentler refresh
  • Barrel shower, finished to match and built on site

The sauna

Six metres of barrel, opening onto the view

The centrepiece is a six-metre barrel sauna with a one-metre changing room that doubles as a heat and sound trap. The panoramic dome window opens the far end onto the fields, with the seating built into the globe itself, and under-seat lighting runs cool in the changing room and warm in the sauna to give a distinct hot-and-cool feel as you move through.

Inside, the barrel shape carries the heat well, the convection currents circulating rather than pooling at the top the way a straight-edged sauna does. The under-seat lighting gives the interior its glow.

The hot tub

Bubbling away, ready when they are

An electric hot tub with enough hydro jets to do the trick, a colour-changing LED that shifts the mood after dark, and a thermo lid to hold the heat between sessions. Sized for relaxed use rather than crowds, so a few people can sink in and unwind at the end of the circuit.

The cold plunges

One bracing, one a refresh

Two double plunges, each seating two, and each on its own chiller and filter, so one can run cold and brave while the other sits as a gentler refresh. A sand filter and thermo lid keep the water fresh between sessions, with no need to empty after every use, so the suite holds a good degree of autonomy when unmanned.

The barrel shower

A rinse between the heat and the cold

A barrel shower in matching thermowood, built on site, giving people a place to rinse off as they move between the sauna, the plunge and the tub. It keeps the plunge water cleaner for longer and rounds out the circuit.

Specification

The detail behind each piece

PieceDetail
Six-metre barrel sauna2.3m wide · 1m changing room · panoramic dome window · under-seat LEDs both sectionsHarvia Cilindro WiFi, ~12kW
Hot tubElectric · hydro jets · colour-changing LED · thermo lidSized for relaxed use
Cold plunge, bracingDouble, seats two · chiller-filter unit · sand filter · thermo lid · independently set1 to 40°C range
Cold plunge, refreshDouble, seats two · chiller-filter unit · sand filter · thermo lid · independently set1 to 40°C range
Barrel showerMatching thermowood · built on siteRinse-before-use station
Materials & lifespan

48mm treated thermowood with an annual paraffin-oil coat, thick stainless tightening bands, no corner joints. A twenty-five-year timber lifespan with light yearly maintenance. The plunges run on a filter and chiller rather than a full empty after each use, so the suite holds a good degree of autonomy when unmanned.

Build subtotal£36,500

All figures exclude VAT. Delivery and installation are a separate line, below.

Delivery & installation

£2,000 to £3,000

A full day of hiab hire to place the sauna, with the hot tub, both plunges and the shower materials brought on one trailer and built on site. Bringing everything in a single load keeps this to one delivery rather than several. Confirmed once the site access and final load are set.

Payment

How payment works

Because every piece is custom-made to commission, payment for the build is made upfront, less the VAT and less the delivery cost. The VAT and the delivery are then settled on the day of delivery.

In short

Build value, less VAT and less delivery, is paid upfront to begin the commission.

The VAT and the delivery cost are paid on delivery.

Next steps

From here

Come and have a feel

Visit the yard and workshop in Caerphilly to see and sit inside the saunas, look over shingle and finish samples, and settle the layout: where the suite orientates, how the view is framed, which finishes you want.

Confirm the build

We lock the specification and finishes, and the upfront payment begins the commission and your place in the queue.

Built and ready

Made to order in the workshop, then delivered and built on site to suit your January opening.

On timing

Order at summer lead times, open in January

Right now the build window is the shortest it will be all year, around ten weeks. Come September and October, as everyone orders at once, that lead time climbs into the months. Opening in January means the two can be made to work in your favour rather than against.

Commit at today's lead time and we build everything ready, hold it in storage at no charge, and deliver on short notice whenever you are ready in the new year, a week or two's word and we organise the lot.

The earlier the order, the shorter the build and the calmer the run-up to opening.
